Slot: WAIST
AC: 1
WT: 1.3 Size: SMALL

Uploaded March 29th, 2023 by iventheassassin
Send a correction
Post Comment
On the progression servers (Ragefire/Lockjaw)
QuoteReply
# Jun 03 2015 at 9:25 PM Rating: Decent
Trellium
508 posts
On the progression servers (Ragefire/Lockjaw) these give experience, gold, decent faction, and various small tattered gear.

For a single character, giving 30 belts to Canloe takes you from 0%, to 5% in to level 5.

For a group of two (both starting at 0% experience) in which each turns in 21 belts.will go all the way to Level as well (about 3% in to level 5).
▲ Page top
Xp ratio
QuoteReply
# Jan 06 2007 at 1:32 AM Rating: Decent
shadowsedge
Scholar
298 posts
On a level 10 rogue, got about 3% xp per 2 belts turned in. Compared to turning in 2 crushbone sholderpads for 10% per 2. Tho when I first started it seemed way higher but memory is strange, you remember the good as better and the better as great and usually forget the bad all together.
____________________________
I know you believe you understand what you think I said, but I am not sure you realize that what you thought you heard was not what I meant.
▲ Page top
Quest Information
QuoteReply
# Jul 30 2005 at 7:49 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592261619086
15 posts
Quest info:
You say, 'Hail, Canloe Nusback'
Canloe Nusback says 'Step forward and speak up, young Kluiana! Kaladim can always use another warrior. Are you [ready to serve Kaladim] or has a yellow streak appeared down your back?'
You say, 'I am ready to serve Kaladim'
Canloe Nusback says 'Then serve you shall. Let your training begin on the battlefields of Faydwer. Seek out and destroy all [Crushbone orcs]. Return their belts to me. I shall also reward you for every two orc legionnaire shoulder pads returned. A warrior great enough to slay one legionnaire shall surely have no problem with another. Go, and let the cleansing of Faydwer begin.'
You say, 'what Crushbone orcs?'
Canloe Nusback says 'The army of the Crushbone orcs is deadly indeed. They are great military strategists. It was a legion of them that brought down the great [Trondle Ogrebane]. Speak with Furtog Ogrebane about the Crushbones. He has need of warriors such as you.'
You say, 'who is trondle ogerbane?'
Canloe Nusback says 'Trondle Ogrebane is the legendary dwarven warrior who single-handedly exterminated the ogre clan called the [Mudtoes]. He was recently killed in battle. It took an entire legion of Crushbone orcs to bring him down. Furtog is still fuming about that.'
You say, 'who are the mudtoes?'
Canloe Nusback says 'The Mudtoes were a small clan of ogres. They lived somewhere in the Butcherblock Mountains. They had an insatiable appetite for dwarves. They were finally destroyed by the hand of Trondle Ogrebane.'

Hand him one crushbone belt

Canloe Nusback says 'Good work, warrior! Now continue with your training. Only on the battlefield can one become a great warrior.'
Your faction standing with Storm Guard got better.
Your faction standing with Kazon Stormhammer got better.
Your faction standing with Miners Guild 249 got better.
Your faction standing with Merchants of Kaladim got better.
Your faction standing with Craknek Warriors got worse.

QuoteReply
# Jan 30 2005 at 11:36 PM Rating: Default

18 posts
The XP has been NERFED

Level 2 High Elf Paladin 1 blue bub into 2

40 Belts

Barely dinged 30


idiots.. this quest should have been left alone!
▲ Page top
Specific Experience Stats
QuoteReply
# Jun 10 2004 at 9:09 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1591808738951
13 posts
At lvl 13, I took 16 crushbone shoulderpads to Kaladim and turned them in.

I am a Dwarven Paladin.

Each shoulderpad gave me 6% exp! (6 blue ticks) I raised almost the entire level off of this.

*Note* the belts, when turned in, give 1% (1 blue tick) at this level.

Gunlok regarded me as Amiable. Just a suggestion. Since so many people do this quest, if there are those of you out there who will post your own results, we can compare differences to determine how quest exp works.

For example, if a lvl 10 paladin Dwarf does it, and gets 9%, that would be good to know.

If a lvl 12 paladin who has poor faction with Gunlok does the quest and gets 2%, that would be VERY good to know.

If a lvl 12 paladin ELF does it and gets 3%, that would be VERY good to know. (racial modifier to exp reward)

If a lvl 12 dwarf FIGHTER does it and gets 12%, that would be good to know (class bonus modifier.)

Be sure and post your Class, Race, Level, and Faction with Gunlok.

It is fairly safe to assume that once the research is done for this quest's experience, the rest of the questing experience for other quests works on exactly the same premise.

(fyi - turning in 4 bone chips at this level/Class/Race/Amiable to Kaladim Pally GM is worth only 1/2%)


"Virt" lvl 12 Paladin Roleplayer - Zeb Server
▲ Page top
Levelling Alts
QuoteReply
# Apr 22 2004 at 6:01 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591803026543
View User Forum
Scholar
33 posts
If you are starting an alt out("good" race), and are a class with at least one spell at lvl 1, this quest is excellent. Have a higher level alt or your main farm these belts, or if you are willing to buy the belts at 1p each or 2p per shoulderpads. Keep farming/buying until you have all of your possible inventory filled up(assuming you have space). Once this is done, go to a bank, put two bags full of em into the shared bank slots, log onto your alt, transfer those at kaladim bank, log back on main, shared bank..and so on until all of your belts/shoulderpads are transfered, then turn in the belts/shoulderpads to the Warrior GM in Kaladim A. Once you have turned in all of your belts/shoulderpads, you should have gained about 5-8 levels from level one (depending on how many shoulderpads over belts you have). After you have traded all of your belts/shoulderpads, go to PoK (plane of knowledge) and start casting spells on yourself (low level spells) until you seem to be at a high skill level for your level (in PoK spells do not harm you or other players if harmful, beneficiary spells do work though). If you are not pure caster, you should probably work on some melee skills as well, put ALL of your points gained from leveling into your melee skill of choice (1hb, 1hs, 2hb, 2hs, pierce). If your skill level for your melee skill is not at a level you are comfortable with, go kill newbie level mobs or join a group and get some xp while training your melle skill.

Repeat this process until you are at a level where CB belts and Shoulderpads will not give you much experience (between lvl 10 -12).

good luck.

Edited, Thu Apr 22 19:04:37 2004
▲ Page top
for ogres
QuoteReply
# Apr 09 2004 at 3:17 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592761705222
7 posts
For you ogre warriors and evil folk, I was able to complete this quest with 8 full backpacks of belts and pads once I killed enough orcs and goblins and got my faction to dubious so I could enter town. As an ogre warrior level 15 I went to 16.5 and my faction went from dubious to amiable with the Storm Guard. The items and coin were just a sideline at this level.

Extremely Easy Money
QuoteReply
# Nov 21 2002 at 4:56 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591783907912
6 posts
I don't understand why people would pay 1pp for a belt. I do the quest myself once in a while for faction.

That may be why people pay so much. It's a quick way to raise faction with a few groups.

*shrugs* i'm not sure.

▲ Page top
RE: Extremely Easy Money
QuoteReply
# Jan 27 2004 at 5:59 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591791519308
11 posts
Another reason why people buy these, is for exp!!! you get massive exp with these and the shoulderpads at low levels. It is a great way to power lvl your toon from lvl 2-10, or higher, but it will take more belts and pads. Just some FYI
▲ Page top
RE: Extremely Easy Money
QuoteReply
# Feb 07 2003 at 2:09 PM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1594570664193
Scholar
245 posts
Well, here's an example. My level 52 Paladin needs Merchants of Kaladim faction to complete an important quest, and these belts are one of the very few ways that Merchants of Kaladim faction can be raised. So, to me it's worth 1 plat per belt because it saves time in raising faction.

That's one of the problems with the overall quest system. Many time higher level folks need to go back and do quests designed for newbies, and what's worse need to do the same quest many, many times to get the faction needed. My Pally is still working on raising Merchants of Kaladim faction. To do this he has to kill spawns that are also needed by newbies hunting in Crushbone. It's much better for the newbies if he buys some of the belts he needs from them, but it's also bad for the newbies because they lose spawns that are appropriate for their level.

Kamgar
▲ Page top
wellity
QuoteReply
# Sep 01 2002 at 4:15 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592255467888
185 posts
well you could sell these to those who might have massive faction issues... i sold 20 belts and 10 cb pads and scored 45pp...

BTW belts on bertoxx sell usually 10 for 5pp or 1 per 5gp... pads go for 1pp... considering most people just farm CB... go figgy

▲ Page top
Easy Money
QuoteReply
# Jul 03 2002 at 8:21 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591772303284
View User Forum
Scholar
19 posts
Oftentimes, the person buying these actually had a high level character with tons of platinum to spare -- they are using these belts as a quick way to PL their twink so they don;t have to find a friend to PL them. hehe.

1pp each is the standard price on Vazaelle.
▲ Page top
RE: Easy Money
QuoteReply
# Aug 20 2002 at 6:18 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592759167585
270 posts
I don't know why people would just buy a bunch of these and give them to their twink to lvl a whole bunch at once, they would be slapped around by things around their lvl afterwards because their skills would still be lvl 1 at lvl 8 or so. It is better to just collect these yourself and turn them in a backpack or 2 at a time, not try to lvl 5-10 times from these or you'll be a wimp for your lvl. The common rememdy for this is to use most of your training points you get from lvls to put them into skills for more acceptable lvls, but it is better to just raise them up yourself for FREE and not have to use precious training points. I'll never twink people like this for quick lvls, unles it is only 1 or 2 lvls worth.
▲ Page top
any faction quests?
QuoteReply
# Jun 24 2002 at 5:48 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591778711073
52 posts
The only quests listed under crushbone belts are the orc belt quest in misty thicket, and the orc vest quest in freeport. I was told that is a quest involving these belts that will raise my dwarf faction. Can someone please post the npc's name that gives the quest and what zone he is in?
▲ Page top
RE: any faction quests?
QuoteReply
# Aug 02 2002 at 11:55 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592255136828
1 post
Canloe Nusback in the warrior guild in Kaladim is the guy you want. (You can also buy belts from players for an ave. of 1PP per). He also has a follow up one to the belt quest, when you can kill lego's, get their Crushbone Shoulderpads. (You can also buy pads from players for an ave. of 2PP per). You need to hand them to Canloe 2 at a time. Decent exp up to about 19th lvl for me as a cleric. Cheers!

Kinadec Liteheart
Luclin Server
21st Season Dwarf Cleric


Edited, Fri Aug 2 12:50:32 2002
▲ Page top
My 2cps
QuoteReply
# Jun 21 2001 at 9:14 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1591758165483
View User Forum
Scholar
92 posts
These are one of the most sought after items in Faydwer mainly because of their unique ability to advance a character extremely rapidly through the first few levels. I had a friend powerleveling me from about levels 1-8. During that time I collected 6 bags of these belts. I turned them all in and I was halfway through level 9 without any effort. Also when you turn them in you get armor from the guy which you can then sell.
▲ Page top

Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
I have a question. I've seen then on the aution block for (usually) 1pp. Why would someone pay 1pp for a belt? I checked and there are a couple of quests that involve them, but that doesn't seem to be worth a PP by itself.
Granted I haven't been playing that long and haven't found the secret to making money yet, but what are they good for?
